factors.summary: A credit rating downgrade could increase borrowing costs.

evidence.text: A downgrade in our credit ratings would increase the cost of borrowings under our CP program and Revolver, and could limit or, in the case of a significant downgrade, preclude our ability to issue CP.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: A credit rating downgrade would increase borrowing costs and potentially limit access to capital, posing a significant financial risk.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in Workforce Solutions and USIS.

evidence.text: Revenue increased by $102.8 million, or 12%, and by $214.7 million, or 12%, in the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019. Total revenue was negatively impacted by foreign exchange rates, which decreased revenue by $13.7 million and $29.6 million, or 2%, for the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019. Revenue in the second quarter and first six months of 2020 increased due to growth in our Workforce Solutions and USIS, primarily due to strong U.S. mortgage volume.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth in higher-margin segments like Workforce Solutions and USIS indicates a strategic shift towards more profitable areas.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are Workforce Solutions, particularly in unemployment claims management, while commercial, financial services, and telecommunications are declining.

evidence.text: In the United States, consolidated revenue grew during the three months ended June 30, 2020, compared to the three months ended June 30 2019, reflecting very strong mortgage market related revenue in both USIS and Workforce Solutions, and very strong revenue growth in our Workforce Solutions unemployment claims management business. However, in the US, we experienced year-over-year revenue declines in most other vertical markets including commercial, financial services and telecommunications. Internationally, all countries in which we operate experienced revenue declines, across most vertical markets.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declines in commercial, financial services, and telecommunications offset growth in Workforce Solutions, indicating broader market challenges.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in royalty and production costs, and technology and data security costs.

evidence.text: The increases were due to an increase in royalty and production costs, as well as incremental technology and data security costs related to our ongoing technology transformation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising costs in royalties, production, and technology security could压缩 profit margins.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with slight increases in people and technology costs, but overall stable.

evidence.text: Selling, general and administrative expenses increased $3.1 million and decreased $679.6 million in the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019. The slight increase in the second quarter was due to an increase in people and technology costs, partially offset by a decrease in legal expense due to a prior year legal accrual that did not recur, and lower business travel costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Slight increases are offset by decreases in other areas, showing overall stability.

factors.summary: Operational efficiency is improving through the ongoing technology transformation, with no significant disruptions.

evidence.text: To date, the change in working environment has not caused material disruptions in the execution of this plan.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvements in operational efficiency without significant disruptions suggest a positive trend.

factors.summary: Management is cautious about future growth due to the uncertain effects of the global economy caused by COVID-19.

evidence.text: Although consolidated revenue has grown during the first half of 2020 when compared to 2019, due to the uncertain effects of the global economy caused by the impact of COVID-19, there can be no assurances that revenues will continue to grow in the second half of 2020.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management's cautious outlook due to COVID-19's uncertain effects signals potential challenges ahead.

factors.summary: Management is cautious, focusing on cost management and maintaining business continuity despite the challenges posed by COVID-19.

evidence.text: Our critical priorities are: (i) the health and safety of our employees and their families; (ii) providing support to consumers; (iii) helping our customers execute their changing business plans by providing innovative solutions combining our unique data assets and leading analytical and technology capabilities; and (iv) executing on our EFX2020 cloud technology, data and security transformation per our previously stated plans.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While cautious, management is focused on cost management and continuity, showing resilience but no clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include the uncertain economic impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and potential future asset impairment charges.

evidence.text: Recovery from the COVID-19 induced recession remains highly uncertain and may require several years to return to economic levels experienced prior to the pandemic and may affect certain markets or regions we serve differently. Any future asset impairment charges, increase in allowance for doubtful accounts, or restructuring charges could be more likely and will be dependent on the severity and duration of this crisis.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The summary highlights significant near-term risks from the COVID-19 pandemic and potential asset impairment charges, indicating challenges ahead.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on cloud technology, data, and security transformation.

evidence.text: Our technology teams continue to execute on our EFX2020 cloud technology, data and security transformation, including the continued migration of our technology to cloud native environments. To date, the change in working environment has not caused material disruptions in the execution of this plan.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on cloud technology, data, and security transformation, which positions it for future growth.

factors.summary: The company is sensitive to foreign exchange rates, which have negatively impacted revenue and costs.

evidence.text: Total revenue was negatively impacted by foreign exchange rates, which decreased revenue by $13.7 million and $29.6 million, or 2%, for the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is sensitive to foreign exchange rates, which have negatively impacted revenue and costs, indicating a vulnerability.

factors.summary: The company's technology transformation and strong performance in certain segments are catalysts for future growth.

evidence.text: Our technology teams continue to execute on our EFX2020 cloud technology, data and security transformation, including the continued migration of our technology to cloud native environments. To date, the change in working environment has not caused material disruptions in the execution of this plan.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The technology transformation and strong performance in certain segments are catalysts for future growth, suggesting positive developments.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to remain at similar levels as in 2019, supporting the technology transformation.

evidence.text: We expect to continue to incur significant expenses and capital expenditures in the second half of 2020 related to these initiatives, at similar levels as those incurred in 2019.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are expected to remain stable, neither indicating a clear positive nor negative trend.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are stable with no share repurchases and consistent dividend payouts.

evidence.text: We did not repurchase shares of our common stock during the first six months of 2020. At June 30, 2020, $590.1 million was available for future purchases of common stock under our share repurchase authorization. We paid out $94.6 million or $0.78 per share in dividends to our shareholders during the first six months of 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns are stable with consistent dividends and no repurchases, showing no clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are focused on joint ventures and investments in emerging markets, which are creating value by expanding capabilities.

evidence.text: We also offer Equifax branded credit services in Russia through a joint venture, have investments in consumer and/or commercial credit information companies through joint ventures in Cambodia, Malaysia, Singapore, and the United Arab Emirates, and have an investment in a consumer and commercial credit information company in Brazil.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Mergers and acquisitions are focused on joint ventures and investments in emerging markets, which are creating value by expanding capabilities.

factors.summary: R&D investment is increasing as part of the technology transformation initiatives, focusing on cloud-native environments.

evidence.text: Our technology teams continue to execute on our EFX2020 cloud technology, data and security transformation, including the continued migration of our technology to cloud native environments. To date, the change in working environment has not caused material disruptions in the execution of this plan.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: R&D investment is increasing as part of the technology transformation initiatives, focusing on cloud-native environments, which is a positive sign for innovation and growth.

factors.summary: The company is adopting digital technologies and automation, particularly in its technology transformation, which is enhancing operational efficiency.

evidence.text: Our technology teams continue to execute on our EFX2020 cloud technology, data and security transformation, including the continued migration of our technology to cloud native environments. To date, the change in working environment has not caused material disruptions in the execution of this plan.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is enhancing operational efficiency through digital technologies and automation.

factors.summary: New products are being developed, particularly in data and analytics, to meet changing customer needs during the pandemic.

evidence.text: Our data and analytics, product and sales teams are focused on how to refine existing products and services, as well as generate new products and services, to meet changing needs of our customer in this environment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is developing new products to meet changing customer needs, indicating adaptability.

factors.summary: The company is managing its workforce with cautious hiring and reduced travel, indicating a defensive stance.

evidence.text: We have significantly limited the addition of new employees and third party contracted services, eliminated all travel except where necessary to meet customer or regulatory needs, and acted to limit discretionary spending.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is cautious with hiring and reducing travel, indicating a defensive stance.

factors.summary: The company is effectively utilizing free cash flow for dividend payments and stock repurchases, while also demonstrating improvement in free cash flow generation, supported by strong operating cash flow.

evidence.text: At June 30, 2020, the Company had $590.1 million remaining for stock repurchases under the existing Board authorization.
Cash provided by operating activities $ 251.2    $ 217.0    $ 282.0    $ 248.0

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Effective use of free cash flow for dividends and repurchases, with improved generation.

factors.summary: The economic conditions have been negatively impacted by the COVID-19 pandemic, leading to reduced demand and revenue across various segments of the business. The challenges are expected to persist until economic improvement occurs.

evidence.text: We have experienced significant revenue declines in several of our markets since the designation of COVID-19 as a global pandemic in March 2020. We expect that the negative impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic on our operating revenue will continue until economic conditions improve.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak.

evidence.text: Starting in the latter half of March, non-mortgage online revenue declined due to the economic impact of COVID-19.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative economic impacts from COVID-19 leading to revenue declines and expected persistence.

factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed impacts with reduced consumer lending activities due to the COVID-19 pandemic, but growth in transaction volumes, particularly in the U.S. mortgage market and Workforce Solutions.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic and the mitigation efforts by governments to attempt to control its spread have adversely impacted the global economy, leading to reduced consumer spending and lending activities and disruptions and volatility in the global capital markets.
evidence.subsection: Our business has been and will continue to be negatively impacted by the recent COVID-19 outbreak.

evidence.text: The increases are due to increased mortgage market transaction volumes.
Revenue in the second quarter and first six months of 2020 increased due to growth in our Workforce Solutions and USIS, primarily due to strong U.S. mortgage volume.

factors.summary: The company has taken steps to enhance its financial flexibility by increasing its leverage ratio. This adjustment has been supported by both the amendment of its revolving credit facility and the maintenance of significant cash reserves.

evidence.text: On April 10, 2020, we amended our existing revolving credit facility to increase the maximum leverage ratio to provide additional financial flexibility.

In the second quarter of 2020, we amended our revolving credit facility to increase the maximum leverage ratio through 2021 to provide us with additional financial flexibility. At June 30, 2020, we had approximately $1.3 billion in cash and $1.3 billion available to borrow under our revolving credit facility that matures in September 2023 and receivables funding facility that matures in December 2022.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Increased leverage for flexibility, but no indication of significant positive or negative impact.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with improvements in mortgage-related services and strong growth in certain areas, but declines in other vertical markets both domestically and internationally.

evidence.text: The increases are due to improvements in our core credit decisioning services volumes related to improvement in the mortgage market.

The impact on the operating results in each country in which we operate differed based on the conditions and the vertical markets we serve in that country. In the United States, consolidated revenue grew during the three months ended June 30, 2020, compared to the three months ended June 30 2019, reflecting very strong mortgage market related revenue in both USIS and Workforce Solutions, and very strong revenue growth in our Workforce Solutions unemployment claims management business. However, in the US, we experienced year-over-year revenue declines in most other vertical markets including commercial, financial services and telecommunications. Internationally, all countries in which we operate experienced revenue declines, across most vertical markets.

factors.summary: The company's margins are experiencing mixed performance. While the operating margin has improved due to higher revenue and lower legal accruals, the International segment is facing pressure from increased costs and economic impacts, leading to a decrease in margins.

evidence.text: The margin decrease during the second quarter and first six months of 2020 was due to increased people, royalty, technology and depreciation costs, as well as incremental technology and data security costs, partially offset by the increase in revenue.

Total company operating margin increased by 4.1 percentage points and 44.8 percentage points in the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019. The increased margins are primarily due to increased revenue and the prior year accrual for losses associated with certain legal proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident that did not recur in 2020, partially offset by increased people, royalty and technology costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The factor shows mixed performance with some improvements and some declines.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, with strong performance in key segments such as Workforce Solutions and USIS. However, foreign exchange rates negatively impacted total revenue by decreasing it by 2% in both the second quarter and first six months of 2020 compared to the same periods in 2019.

evidence.text: Revenue increased by 7% and 11% in the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019.

Revenue increased by $102.8 million, or 12%, and by $214.7 million, or 12%, in the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019. Total revenue was negatively impacted by foreign exchange rates, which decreased revenue by $13.7 million and $29.6 million, or 2%, for the second quarter and first six months of 2020, respectively, compared to the same periods in 2019.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating despite foreign exchange impacts.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction actions, particularly in the International segment and operating expenses, which are showing measurable savings, especially in legal expenses. However, there are ongoing incremental technology costs and local expense management efforts that may offset some of these savings.

evidence.text: The decreased margin in the second quarter and first six months of 2020 was primarily due to the negative effects of COVID-19 on the countries' revenue where Equifax operates, as well as incremental technology costs related to the ongoing technology transformation, partially offset by discretionary expense control across local geographies.

For the six months ended June 30, 2020, the decrease is due to $701.3 million in legal accruals associated with certain legal proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident recorded in the first six months of 2019 that did not recur in 2020, partially offset by an increase in people costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction actions are showing some savings but offset by other costs.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation exposure primarily related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident. While some favorable adjustments have been made, there is still a risk of additional material losses due to ongoing legal proceedings and settlements.

evidence.text: The 2020 year-to-date rate has been adversely impacted by valuation allowances against the deferred tax assets of certain international operations, however this impact was partially offset by favorable adjustments related to accruals for losses associated with certain legal proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident.

We believe it is probable that we will incur losses associated with certain of the proceedings and investigations related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident. In 2019, we recorded expenses, net of insurance recoveries, of $800.9 million in other current liabilities and selling, general, and administrative expenses in our Consolidated Balance Sheets and Statements of Income (Loss), respectively, exclusive of our legal and professional services expenses. The amount accrued represents our best estimate of the liability related to these matters. The Company will continue to evaluate information as it becomes known and adjust accruals for new information and further developments in accordance with ASC 450-20-25. While it is reasonably possible that losses exceeding the amount accrued may be incurred, it is not possible at this time to estimate the additional possible loss in excess of the amount already accrued that might result from adverse judgments, settlements, penalties or other resolution of the proceedings and investigations described below based on a number of factors, such as the various stages of these proceedings and investigations, including matters on appeal, that alleged damages have not been specified or are uncertain, the uncertainty as to the certification of a class or classes and the size of any certified class, as applicable, and the lack of resolution on significant factual and legal issues. The ultimate amount paid on these actions, claims and investigations in excess of the amount already accrued could be material to the Company’s consolidated financial condition, results of operations, or cash flows in future periods.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant ongoing litigation exposure with potential for material losses.
